title: Multimodal Buyer Simulator Agent
description: An AI agent that simulates human purchase behavior for e-commerce product selection using a multimodal LLM, structured outputs, and optional retrieval-augmented grounding.
problem: Predicting whether a buyer will purchase a product requires understanding visual appeal, pricing context, and subjective preferences. Traditional rule-based or tabular approaches miss the multimodal reasoning that drives real purchase decisions. The goal was to simulate a buyer's decision process by combining product images, titles, and prices into a single evaluation pass through a multimodal LLM.
approach: Built an agent workflow that accepts a product title, price, and image, passes them through a multimodal LLM, and returns a structured purchase probability with rationale. The pipeline optionally retrieves similar historical product examples to ground the agent's reasoning, improving calibration and consistency.
approachBullets: Multimodal LLM evaluates product title, price, and image in a single pass
Structured output schema enforces purchase_probability and rationale fields
Optional retrieval of similar historical examples to ground decisions (RAG)
Prompt evaluation checklist and iterative prompt tuning for consistency
Caching and reproducibility considerations for repeatable evaluations
results: TODO: add offline evaluation metrics (AUC, Precision@K)
TODO: add calibration results comparing agent vs. baseline
TODO: add latency and cost benchmarks per evaluation
Structured, explainable outputs with purchase probability and reasoning
systemDesign: Product Input (title + price + image) -> Retriever (FAISS index of historical products) -> Prompt Assembly (product context + retrieved examples + evaluation instructions) -> Multimodal LLM (OpenAI GPT-4o) -> Structured Output Parser (Pydantic schema) -> Decision Output (purchase_probability + rationale)
nextSteps: Run offline evaluation against labeled purchase datasets
Add calibration analysis comparing predicted probabilities to actual outcomes
Experiment with different retrieval strategies and embedding models
Test prompt variations systematically with an evaluation harness
Explore fine-tuning a smaller model on the structured output format

title: Mobility & Logistics-Adjacent Analytics
description: Engineered scalable SQL pipelines to analyze large regional mobility datasets, identifying bottlenecks, efficiency patterns, and growth signals relevant to logistics and operational planning.
problem: Large regional mobility datasets contained hidden patterns around bottlenecks, efficiency, and growth signals that required scalable analytical pipelines to surface actionable insights for logistics and operational planning.
approach: Engineered scalable SQL pipelines in BigQuery, performed trend analysis and KPI-style metrics to support data-driven decisions across regions and time periods. Structured queries and outputs for repeatable reporting and future operational analysis.
results: Scalable SQL pipelines for large regional mobility datasets
Identified bottlenecks, efficiency patterns, and growth signals
KPI-style metrics supporting data-driven decisions across regions
Repeatable reporting structure for future operational analysis

title: Visual Analytics – Operational Opportunity Assessment
description: Analyzed large-scale datasets to uncover trends and inefficiencies, designing executive-ready visualizations to communicate insights clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
problem: Complex analytical findings buried in large-scale datasets were difficult for non-technical stakeholders to understand, slowing down decision-making and obscuring key drivers and opportunity gaps.
approach: Analyzed large-scale datasets to uncover trends and inefficiencies. Applied best practices in data storytelling and dashboard design to highlight key drivers and opportunity gaps. Designed executive-ready visualizations that simplify complex findings into intuitive visuals.
results: Uncovered trends and inefficiencies in large-scale datasets
Faster stakeholder understanding through intuitive dashboard design
Highlighted key drivers and opportunity gaps

title: Data Mining – Predictive Analytics Project
description: Performed advanced data cleaning, feature engineering, and trend analysis on high-dimensional datasets, building and evaluating predictive models with emphasis on stability and decision relevance.
problem: High-dimensional datasets required careful cleaning, feature engineering, and rigorous model evaluation to produce stable, generalizable predictions that could reliably support business decisions.
approach: Performed advanced data cleaning, feature engineering, and trend analysis emphasizing model stability, data quality, and decision relevance. Built and evaluated predictive models using cross-validation and performance metrics to ensure generalizability. Documented assumptions, limitations, and findings for transparent analytics.
results: Predictive models validated with cross-validation and performance metrics
Advanced feature engineering on high-dimensional datasets
Transparent, decision-ready analytics with documented assumptions
Emphasis on model stability and generalizability

title: BudgetBite – Product & Process Analytics
description: Applied data analysis and experimentation techniques to study user behavior, translating insights into measurable improvements in usability and operational efficiency.
problem: Understanding user behavior patterns, engagement drivers, and drop-off points was critical for improving usability and operational efficiency in the BudgetBite product.
approach: Applied data analysis and experimentation techniques to study user behavior. Conducted pattern and cohort analysis to identify drivers of engagement and drop-off. Framed analytical insights into product and process recommendations grounded in business impact.
results: Measurable improvements in usability and operational efficiency
Pattern and cohort analysis revealing engagement drivers and drop-off
Product and process recommendations grounded in business impact
